hierarchy.text:
<image>
RBI/2021-22/88
FMRD.DIRD.10/14.03.004/2021-22


 February 10, 2022 (Updated as on January 01, 2025)

To,
All Eligible Market Participants
Madam/Sir,
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2022
Please refer to Paragraph 4 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory
Policies announced as a part of the Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement for 2021-22
dated February 10, 2022, regarding review of the Credit Default Swaps (CDS)
Guidelines.

hierarchy.title: 2. The draft Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2021 were released
hierarchy.text:
2. The draft Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2021 were released
for public comments on February 16, 2021. Based on the feedback received from the
market participants, the draft Directions were reviewed and have since been finalised.
The Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2022 are
enclosed herewith.
Yours faithfully,
(Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
FINANCIAL MARKETS REGULATION DEPARTMENT
Notification No. FMRD.DIRD.11/14.03.004/2021-22 dated February 10, 2022
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2022
(Updated as on January 01, 2025)
In exercise of the powers conferred under section 45W of the Reserve Bank of India
Act, 1934 (02 of 1934) (hereinafter called the Act) read with section 45U of the Act and
in supersession of Circular No. IDMD.PCD.No.10/14.03.04/2012-13 dated January 07,
2013, the Reserve Bank of India (hereinafter called the Reserve Bank) hereby issues
the following Directions.
A reference is also invited to the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of
1999), Foreign Exchange Management (Debt Instruments) Regulations, 2019
(Notification No. FEMA. 396/2019-RB dated October 17, 2019) and A.P. (DIR Series)
Circular No. 23 dated February 10, 2022 on Transactions in Credit Default Swap (CDS)
by Foreign Portfolio Investors - Operational Instructions.

children.title: (i) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
(i) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Credit Derivatives) Directions, 2022.

children.title: (ii) These Directions shall apply to credit derivatives transactions undertaken in Over-
children.text:
(ii) These Directions shall apply to credit derivatives transactions undertaken in Over-
the-Counter (OTC) markets and on recognised stock exchanges in India.

children.title: (iii) These Directions shall come into force on May 09, 2022.
children.text: (iii) These Directions shall come into force on May 09, 2022.

children.title: (i) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:
children.text: (i) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:

children.title: (a) 'Auction settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the price of
children.text:
(a) 'Auction settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the price of
the reference/deliverable obligation at which the settlement will happen is
determined through an auction mechanism.

children.title: (b) 'Cash settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the protection
children.text:
(b) 'Cash settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the protection
seller pays to the protection buyer, the notional amount of the CDS contract
less the expected recovery value of the reference obligation.

children.title: (c) 'Central counterparty' means an entity that interposes itself between
children.text:
(c) 'Central counterparty' means an entity that interposes itself between
counterparties to contracts traded in one or more financial markets, becoming

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
the buyer to every seller and the seller to every buyer and thereby ensuring the
performance of open contracts.

children.title: (d) 'Company' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(20) of
children.text:
(d) 'Company' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(20) of
the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).

children.title: (e) 'Corporate bonds and debentures' mean non-convertible debt securities which
children.text:
(e) 'Corporate bonds and debentures' mean non-convertible debt securities which
create or acknowledge indebtedness and include debentures, bonds and such
other securities issued by a body corporate or a trust or any statutory body
constituted by virtue of a legislation, whether constituting a charge on the
assets of the issuer or not, but excludes money market debt instruments,
security receipts, securitized debt instruments and bonds issued by the Central
Government or a State Government, or such other persons as may be
specified by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (f) 'Credit Default Swap (CDS)' means a credit derivative contract in which one
children.text:
(f) 'Credit Default Swap (CDS)' means a credit derivative contract in which one
counterparty (protection seller) commits to pay to the other counterparty
(protection buyer) in the case of a credit event with respect to a reference entity
and in return, the protection buyer makes periodic payments (premium) to the
protection seller until the maturity of the contract or the credit event, whichever
is earlier.

children.title: (g) 'Credit derivative' means a derivative contract whose value is derived from the
children.text:
(g) 'Credit derivative' means a derivative contract whose value is derived from the
credit risk of an underlying debt instrument.

children.title: (h) 'Credit event' means a pre-defined event in a credit derivative contract, which
children.text:
(h) 'Credit event' means a pre-defined event in a credit derivative contract, which
triggers a settlement under the contract.

children.title: (i) 'Deliverable obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
children.text:
(i) 'Deliverable obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
that the protection buyer can deliver to the protection seller in a physically
settled CDS contract, in case of occurrence of a credit event.

children.title: (j) 'Electronic Trading Platform (ETP)' shall have the same meaning as assigned
children.text:
(j) 'Electronic Trading Platform (ETP)' shall have the same meaning as assigned
to it in Paragraph 2(1)(iii) of The Electronic Trading Platforms (Reserve Bank)
Directions, 2018 issued vide Notification No. FMRD.FMID.08/2018 dated
October 05, 2018, as amended from time to time.

children.title: (k) 'Exchange' shall mean 'recognised stock exchange' and shall have the same
children.text:
(k) 'Exchange' shall mean 'recognised stock exchange' and shall have the same
meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(f) of the Securities Contract Regulation
Act, 1956 (42 of 1956).

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (l) 'Government-related entity' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
children.text:
(l) 'Government-related entity' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in
Paragraph 9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24: Related Party
Disclosures.

children.title: (m) 'Hedging' means the activity of undertaking a credit derivative transaction to
children.text:
(m) 'Hedging' means the activity of undertaking a credit derivative transaction to
reduce credit risk of a particular debt instrument or a portfolio of debt
instruments.

children.title: (n) 'Infrastructure company' means a company engaged primarily in activities
children.text:
(n) 'Infrastructure company' means a company engaged primarily in activities
related to specific infrastructure sub-sectors listed under the Harmonized
Master List of Infrastructure Sub-sectors notified by Department of Economic
Affairs, Ministry of Finance, Government of India, via gazette notification no.
F.No.13/6/2009-INF dated April 08, 2016, as amended from time to time.

children.title: (o) 'Market-maker' means an entity which provides prices to users and other
children.text:
(o) 'Market-maker' means an entity which provides prices to users and other
market-makers.

children.title: (p) 'Money market debt instruments' mean commercial papers as defined under
children.text:
(p) 'Money market debt instruments' mean commercial papers as defined under
the Reserve Bank Commercial Paper Directions, 2017, issued vide Notification
No. FMRD.DIRD.2/14.01.002/2017-18 dated August 10, 2017 as amended
from time to time, certificates of deposit as defined under the Master Direction

children.title: - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021, issued vide
children.text:
- Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021, issued vide
Notification No. FMRD.DIRD.03/14.01.003/2021-22 dated June 04, 2021 as
amended from time to time, and non-convertible debentures of original or initial
maturity up to one year as defined under the Master Direction on Money
Market Instruments: Call/Notice Money Market, Commercial Paper, Certificates
of Deposit and Non-Convertible Debentures (original maturity up to one year),
issued vide Notification No. FMRD. Master Direction No.2/2016-17 dated July
07, 2016 as amended from time to time.

children.title: (q) 'Net worth' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(57) of
children.text:
(q) 'Net worth' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(57) of
the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).

children.title: (r) 'Non-resident' shall mean a 'person resident outside India' and shall have the
children.text:
(r) 'Non-resident' shall mean a 'person resident outside India' and shall have the
same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2(w) of Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (s) 'Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets' mean the markets where transactions are
children.text:
(s) 'Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets' mean the markets where transactions are
undertaken in any manner other than on exchanges and shall include
electronic trading platforms (ETPs).

children.title: (t) 'Physical settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the
children.text:
(t) 'Physical settlement' of CDS means a settlement process in which the
protection buyer delivers any of the eligible deliverable obligations to the
protection seller against the receipt of notional amount of the CDS contract.

children.title: (u) 'Reference entity' means an entity, against whose credit risk, a credit derivative
children.text:
(u) 'Reference entity' means an entity, against whose credit risk, a credit derivative
contract is entered into.

children.title: (v) 'Reference obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
children.text:
(v) 'Reference obligation' means a debt instrument issued by the reference entity
and specified in a CDS contract for the purpose of valuation of the contract and
for determining the cash settlement value or the deliverable obligation in case
of occurrence of a credit event.

children.title: (w) 'Related party' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Paragraph
children.text:
(w) 'Related party' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Paragraph
9 of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures.

children.title: (x) 'Resident' shall mean a 'person resident in India' and shall have the same
children.text:
(x) 'Resident' shall mean a 'person resident in India' and shall have the same
meaning as assigned to it in section 2(v) of the Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

children.title: (y) 'Single-name CDS' means a CDS contract in which the underlying is a single
children.text:
(y) 'Single-name CDS' means a CDS contract in which the underlying is a single
reference entity.

children.title: (z) 'Substitution event' means any event that results in the reference obligation
children.text:
(z) 'Substitution event' means any event that results in the reference obligation
being replaced by another obligation issued by the reference entity.
(aa) 'Succession event' means any event that results in the reference entity no
longer being the primary obligor for the reference obligation.
(bb) 'User' means a person that undertakes derivative transactions other than as a
market-maker.

children.title: (ii) Words and expressions, used but not defined in these Directions, shall have the
children.text:
(ii) Words and expressions, used but not defined in these Directions, shall have the
same meaning as assigned to them in the Act.

children.title: (i) The following persons shall be eligible to participate in credit derivatives market:
children.text: (i) The following persons shall be eligible to participate in credit derivatives market:

children.title: (a) Residents; and
children.text: (a) Residents; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (b) Non-residents, who are eligible to invest in corporate bonds and debentures
children.text:
(b) Non-residents, who are eligible to invest in corporate bonds and debentures
under the Foreign Exchange Management (Debt Instruments) Regulations,
2019 dated October 17, 2019, as amended from time to time.

hierarchy.title: 4. Permitted products in the OTC market
hierarchy.text: 4. Permitted products in the OTC market

children.title: (i) Market-makers and users may undertake transactions in single-name CDS
children.text:
(i) Market-makers and users may undertake transactions in single-name CDS
contracts.

hierarchy.title: 5. Market-makers and users in the OTC market
hierarchy.text: 5. Market-makers and users in the OTC market

children.title: 5.1 Market-makers
children.text: 5.1 Market-makers

children.title: (i) The following entities shall be eligible to act as market-makers in credit derivatives:
children.text: (i) The following entities shall be eligible to act as market-makers in credit derivatives:

children.title: (a) Scheduled Commercial Banks, except Small Finance Banks, Payment Banks,
children.text:
(a) Scheduled Commercial Banks, except Small Finance Banks, Payment Banks,
Local Area Banks and Regional Rural Banks;

children.title: (b) Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), including Standalone Primary
children.text:
(b) Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), including Standalone Primary
Dealers (SPDs) and Housing Finance Companies (HFCs), with minimum net
owned funds of ₹500 crore as per the audited balance sheet as on March 31 of
the previous financial year and subject to specific approval of the Department
of Regulation, Reserve Bank; and

children.title: (c) Export Import Bank of India, National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
children.text:
(c) Export Import Bank of India, National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
Development, National Housing Bank, Small Industries Development Bank of
India and National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development.

children.title: (ii) In case an NBFC, an SPD or an HFC, fails to meet the eligibility criteria subsequent
children.text:
(ii) In case an NBFC, an SPD or an HFC, fails to meet the eligibility criteria subsequent
to the receipt of approval for acting as a market-maker, it shall cease to act as a
market-maker. The NBFC, SPD or HFC shall continue to meet all its obligations
under the existing contracts till the maturity/termination of such contracts.

children.title: (iii) At least one of the parties to a credit derivative transaction shall be a market-maker
children.text:
(iii) At least one of the parties to a credit derivative transaction shall be a market-maker
or a central counterparty authorised by the Reserve Bank for the purpose.

children.title: 5.2 User Classification Framework
children.text: 5.2 User Classification Framework

children.title: (i) Users shall be classified by market-makers either as retail or non-retail for the
children.text:
(i) Users shall be classified by market-makers either as retail or non-retail for the
purpose of offering credit derivative contracts.

children.title: (ii) The following users shall be eligible to be classified as non-retail users:
children.text: (ii) The following users shall be eligible to be classified as non-retail users:

children.title: (a) NBFCs, including SPDs and HFCs, other than market-makers;
children.text: (a) NBFCs, including SPDs and HFCs, other than market-makers;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (b) Insurance Companies regulated by Insurance Regulatory and Development
children.text:
(b) Insurance Companies regulated by Insurance Regulatory and Development
Authority of India (IRDAI);

children.title: (c) Pension Funds regulated by Pension Fund Regulatory and Development
children.text:
(c) Pension Funds regulated by Pension Fund Regulatory and Development
Authority (PFRDA);

children.title: (d) Mutual Funds regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI);
children.text: (d) Mutual Funds regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI);

children.title: (e) Alternative Investment Funds regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of
children.text:
(e) Alternative Investment Funds regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of
India (SEBI);

children.title: (f) Resident companies with minimum net worth of ₹500 crore as per the latest
children.text:
(f) Resident companies with minimum net worth of ₹500 crore as per the latest
audited balance sheet; and

children.title: (g) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) registered with SEBI.
children.text: (g) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) registered with SEBI.

children.title: (iii) Any user who is not eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall be classified as
children.text:
(iii) Any user who is not eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall be classified as
a retail user.

children.title: (iv) Any user who is otherwise eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall have the
children.text:
(iv) Any user who is otherwise eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall have the
option to get classified as a retail user.

hierarchy.title: 6. Protection buyers and sellers for Credit Default Swaps in OTC market
hierarchy.text: 6. Protection buyers and sellers for Credit Default Swaps in OTC market

children.title: (i) Retail users shall be allowed to buy protection only for the purpose of hedging.
children.text: (i) Retail users shall be allowed to buy protection only for the purpose of hedging.

children.title: (ii) Non-retail users shall be allowed to buy protection for hedging or otherwise.
children.text: (ii) Non-retail users shall be allowed to buy protection for hedging or otherwise.

children.title: (iii) The following non-retail users shall be eligible to act as protection sellers:
children.text: (iii) The following non-retail users shall be eligible to act as protection sellers:

children.title: (a) Insurance Companies regulated by IRDAI;
children.text: (a) Insurance Companies regulated by IRDAI;

children.title: (b) Pension Funds regulated by PFRDA;
children.text: (b) Pension Funds regulated by PFRDA;

children.title: (c) Mutual Funds regulated by SEBI;
children.text: (c) Mutual Funds regulated by SEBI;

children.title: (d) Alternative Investment Funds regulated by SEBI; and
children.text: (d) Alternative Investment Funds regulated by SEBI; and

children.title: (e) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) registered with SEBI.
children.text: (e) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) registered with SEBI.

children.title: (iv) Insurance Companies, Pension Funds, Mutual Funds and Alternative Investment
children.text:
(iv) Insurance Companies, Pension Funds, Mutual Funds and Alternative Investment
Funds mentioned under Paragraph 6(iii) shall be permitted to act as protection
sellers subject to approval of their respective regulator.

children.title: (v) Participation by FPIs shall be subject to the provisions of A.P. (DIR Series) Circular
children.text:
(v) Participation by FPIs shall be subject to the provisions of A.P. (DIR Series) Circular
No. 23 dated February 10, 2022 on Transactions in Credit Default Swap (CDS) by
Foreign Portfolio Investors - Operational Instructions.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: 7. Reference Entities and Obligations for Credit Default Swaps in OTC market
hierarchy.text: 7. Reference Entities and Obligations for Credit Default Swaps in OTC market

children.title: (i) The reference entity in a CDS contract shall be a resident entity who is eligible to
children.text:
(i) The reference entity in a CDS contract shall be a resident entity who is eligible to
issue any of the debt instruments mentioned under Paragraph 7(ii).

children.title: (ii) The following debt instruments issued in India shall be eligible to be a reference
children.text:
(ii) The following debt instruments issued in India shall be eligible to be a reference
obligation in a CDS contract:

children.title: (a) Money market debt instruments;
children.text: (a) Money market debt instruments;

children.title: (b) Rated INR corporate bonds and debentures; and
children.text: (b) Rated INR corporate bonds and debentures; and

children.title: (c) Unrated INR corporate bonds and debentures issued by the Special Purpose
children.text:
(c) Unrated INR corporate bonds and debentures issued by the Special Purpose
Vehicles set up by infrastructure companies.

children.title: (iii) Bonds with call/put options shall be eligible to be reference obligations.
children.text: (iii) Bonds with call/put options shall be eligible to be reference obligations.

children.title: (iv) Asset-backed securities/mortgage-backed securities and structured obligations such
children.text:
(iv) Asset-backed securities/mortgage-backed securities and structured obligations such
as credit enhanced/guaranteed bonds, convertible bonds, etc. shall not be permitted
as reference obligations.

children.title: (v) The reference obligation/deliverable obligation shall be in dematerialised form.
children.text: (v) The reference obligation/deliverable obligation shall be in dematerialised form.

hierarchy.title: 8. Operational Directions for Credit Default Swaps in the OTC market
hierarchy.text: 8. Operational Directions for Credit Default Swaps in the OTC market

children.title: 8.1 Buying, Unwinding and Settlement
children.text: 8.1 Buying, Unwinding and Settlement

children.title: (i) Market participants shall not enter into CDS transactions if the reference entity is a
children.text:
(i) Market participants shall not enter into CDS transactions if the reference entity is a
related party to either the protection buyer or the protection seller. However, two (or
more) government-related entities shall not be deemed as related parties for the
purpose of these Directions. Market-makers shall establish appropriate controls to
ensure that transactions with related parties are carried out on an arm's length
basis.

children.title: (ii) Market participants shall not buy/sell protection on reference entities if there are
children.text:
(ii) Market participants shall not buy/sell protection on reference entities if there are
regulatory restrictions on such participants assuming similar exposures in the cash
market or in violation of any other regulatory restriction, as may be applicable.

children.title: (iii) Market participants can exit their CDS contract by unwinding the contract with the
children.text:
(iii) Market participants can exit their CDS contract by unwinding the contract with the
original counterparty or assigning the contract to any other eligible market
participant through novation1 subject to the provisions of the circular on Novation of
1 Novation is the replacement of a contract between two counterparties to an OTC derivative transaction (the transferor, who steps out of the existing contract, and the remaining party) with a new contract between the remaining party and a third party (the transferee). The transferee becomes the new counterparty to the remaining party.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
OTC Derivative Contracts dated December 9, 2013 issued vide Notification No.
DBOD.No.BP.BC.76/21.04.157/2013-14. However, provisions under Paragraph 2,
Paragraph 5.1 and Paragraph 5.2 of the above circular shall not apply to CDS
transactions undertaken in terms of these Directions.

children.title: (iv) Market participants shall settle CDS contracts bilaterally or through any clearing and
children.text:
(iv) Market participants shall settle CDS contracts bilaterally or through any clearing and
settlement arrangement approved by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (v) CDS contracts can be cash settled, physically settled or settled through auction. The
children.text:
(v) CDS contracts can be cash settled, physically settled or settled through auction. The
procedure for cash settlement and auction settlement shall be determined by the
Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee as specified under Paragraph 9 of
these Directions.

children.title: 8.2 Transactions with retail users
children.text: 8.2 Transactions with retail users

children.title: (i) Market-makers shall ensure that all CDS transactions by retail users are
children.text:
(i) Market-makers shall ensure that all CDS transactions by retail users are
undertaken for the purpose of hedging, i.e. retail users buying protection:

children.title: (a) shall have exposure to any of the eligible reference obligations;
children.text: (a) shall have exposure to any of the eligible reference obligations;

children.title: (b) shall not buy a CDS contract(s) with notional amount higher than the face value
children.text:
(b) shall not buy a CDS contract(s) with notional amount higher than the face value
of the reference obligation held by them; and

children.title: (c) shall not buy a CDS contract with a tenor later than the maturity of the
children.text:
(c) shall not buy a CDS contract with a tenor later than the maturity of the
reference obligation held by them or the standard CDS maturity date
immediately after the maturity of the reference obligation.
To ensure compliance with the above, market-makers may call for any relevant
information/documents from the retail user, who, in turn, shall be obliged to provide
such information.

children.title: (ii) Retail users shall exit their CDS position within one month from the date they cease
children.text:
(ii) Retail users shall exit their CDS position within one month from the date they cease
to have underlying exposure.

children.title: (iii) CDS contracts involving retail users shall be mandatorily physically settled.
children.text: (iii) CDS contracts involving retail users shall be mandatorily physically settled.

children.title: 8.3 Standardisation
children.text: 8.3 Standardisation

children.title: (i) Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India (FIMMDA), in
children.text:
(i) Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India (FIMMDA), in
consultation with market participants and based on international best practices,
shall devise standard master agreement/s for the Indian CDS market which shall,
inter-alia, include credit event definitions and settlement procedures.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) FIMMDA shall, at the minimum, publish the following trading conventions for CDS
children.text:
(ii) FIMMDA shall, at the minimum, publish the following trading conventions for CDS
contracts:

children.title: (a) Standard maturity and premium payment dates;
children.text: (a) Standard maturity and premium payment dates;

children.title: (b) Standard premiums;
children.text: (b) Standard premiums;

children.title: (c) Upfront fee calculation methodology;
children.text: (c) Upfront fee calculation methodology;

children.title: (d) Accrual payment for full first premium;
children.text: (d) Accrual payment for full first premium;

children.title: (e) Quoting conventions; and
children.text: (e) Quoting conventions; and

children.title: (f) Lookback period for credit events.
children.text: (f) Lookback period for credit events.

children.title: 8.4 Documentation
children.text: 8.4 Documentation

children.title: (i) The CDS contracts shall, inter-alia, mention the following:
children.text: (i) The CDS contracts shall, inter-alia, mention the following:

children.title: (a) reference entity, reference obligation and deliverable obligation(s);
children.text: (a) reference entity, reference obligation and deliverable obligation(s);

children.title: (b) credit event definitions; and
children.text: (b) credit event definitions; and

children.title: (c) the method and procedure of settlement.
children.text: (c) the method and procedure of settlement.

children.title: (ii) The CDS contract shall represent a direct claim on the protection seller. The
children.text:
(ii) The CDS contract shall represent a direct claim on the protection seller. The
contract shall not have any clause that may:

children.title: (a) allow the protection seller to unilaterally cancel the contract, except in case of a
children.text:
(a) allow the protection seller to unilaterally cancel the contract, except in case of a
default by the protection buyer under the terms of the contract;

children.title: (b) prevent the protection seller from making the credit event payment in a timely
children.text:
(b) prevent the protection seller from making the credit event payment in a timely
manner, after occurrence of the credit event and completion of necessary
conditions and requirements under the terms of contract; or

children.title: (c) provide the protection seller any recourse to the protection buyer for credit
children.text:
(c) provide the protection seller any recourse to the protection buyer for credit
event losses.

children.title: 8.5 Customer protection
children.text: 8.5 Customer protection

children.title: (i) Market-makers in OTC markets shall comply with the Master Direction - Reserve
children.text:
(i) Market-makers in OTC markets shall comply with the Master Direction - Reserve
Bank of India (Market-makers in OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2021 issued vide RBI
Circular No. FMRD.FMD.07/02.03.247/2021-22 dated September 16, 2021, as
amended from time to time and Reserve Bank of India (Prevention of Market
Abuse) Directions, 2019 issued vide RBI Circular No.
FMRD.FMSD.11/11.01.012/2018-19 dated March 15, 2019, as amended from time
to time.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 8.6 Reporting
children.text: 8.6 Reporting

children.title: (i) Market-makers shall report all OTC CDS transactions within 30 minutes of the
children.text:
(i) Market-makers shall report all OTC CDS transactions within 30 minutes of the
transaction, to the trade repository of Clearing Corporation of India Ltd. (CCIL).

children.title: (ii) Market-makers shall report all unwinding, novation, settlement transactions, and
children.text:
(ii) Market-makers shall report all unwinding, novation, settlement transactions, and
any credit, substitution or succession event to the trade repository of CCIL.

children.title: (i) FIMMDA shall set up a Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, consisting of
children.text:
(i) FIMMDA shall set up a Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, consisting of
market-makers and users in credit derivatives as voting members. FIMMDA shall
ensure that users are adequately represented in the Committee. The Committee
may also include central counterparties as observer members and
legal/audit/consultancy firms as consultative members.

children.title: (ii) FIMMDA shall establish rules for governing the activities of the Credit Derivatives
children.text:
(ii) FIMMDA shall establish rules for governing the activities of the Credit Derivatives
Determinations Committee in line with international best practices.

children.title: (iii) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
children.text:
(iii) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
participants, shall make factual determinations regarding key provisions of credit
derivative contracts including, but not limited to, the occurrence of a credit event,
substitution event, succession event, determining the identity of successor
reference entity, etc.

children.title: (iv) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall, in consultation with market
children.text:
(iv) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall, in consultation with market
participants, develop a standard procedure for cash settlement of CDS contracts.

children.title: (v) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
children.text:
(v) The Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee, when approached by market
participants, may conduct an auction to determine the reference price for
settlement of CDS contracts. In case an auction is conducted, the Credit
Derivatives Determinations Committee shall put in place procedures/safeguards to
ensure that the reference price is determined in a fair and transparent manner.

children.title: (vi) The decisions of the Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall be binding
children.text:
(vi) The decisions of the Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall be binding
on the market participants.

children.title: (i) Exchanges may offer standardised single-name CDS contracts with guaranteed
children.text:
(i) Exchanges may offer standardised single-name CDS contracts with guaranteed
settlement.

children.title: (ii) Exchanges shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank for product design,
children.text:
(ii) Exchanges shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank for product design,
changes in product design, eligible participants and other details of CDS contracts.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iii) Exchanges shall ensure that the participants on exchanges are made adequately
children.text:
(iii) Exchanges shall ensure that the participants on exchanges are made adequately
aware of the risks associated with CDS.

children.title: (iv) The reference entities and reference obligations for exchange-traded CDS shall be
children.text:
(iv) The reference entities and reference obligations for exchange-traded CDS shall be
as specified under Paragraph 7 of these Directions.

children.title: (v) Participants who are retail users, as defined under Paragraph 5.2 of these
children.text:
(v) Participants who are retail users, as defined under Paragraph 5.2 of these
Directions, shall undertake transactions in exchange-traded CDS only for hedging
and such users

children.title: (a) shall have exposure to any of the eligible reference obligations;
children.text: (a) shall have exposure to any of the eligible reference obligations;

children.title: (b) shall not buy a CDS contract(s) for notional amount higher than the face value
children.text:
(b) shall not buy a CDS contract(s) for notional amount higher than the face value
of the reference obligation held by them; and

children.title: (c) shall not buy a CDS contract with a tenor later than the maturity of the
children.text:
(c) shall not buy a CDS contract with a tenor later than the maturity of the
reference obligation held by them or the standard CDS maturity date
immediately after the maturity of the reference obligation.

children.title: (vi) Participants shall not buy/sell protection on reference entities if there are regulatory
children.text:
(vi) Participants shall not buy/sell protection on reference entities if there are regulatory
restrictions on assuming similar exposures in the cash market or in violation of any
other regulatory restriction, as may be applicable.

children.title: (vii) The CDS contracts traded on exchanges can be cash settled or settled through
children.text:
(vii) The CDS contracts traded on exchanges can be cash settled or settled through
auction. The procedure for cash settlement and auction settlement shall be
determined by the Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee as specified under
Paragraph 9 of these Directions.

children.title: (viii) The determinations made by Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall be
children.text:
(viii) The determinations made by Credit Derivatives Determinations Committee shall be
applicable to exchange-traded CDS contracts.

children.title: (ix) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) may transact in exchange-traded CDS as
children.text:
(ix) Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) may transact in exchange-traded CDS as
protection sellers and/or protection buyers. Participation by FPIs shall be subject to
the provisions of A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 23 dated February 10, 2022 on
Transactions in Credit Default Swap (CDS) by Foreign Portfolio Investors -
Operational Instructions. Exchanges shall report the gross notional amount of
protection sold by FPIs to CCIL on a daily basis by the end of the day, or on an
intra-day basis if required by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (x) Exchanges shall report all CDS transactions to the trade repository authorised for
children.text:
(x) Exchanges shall report all CDS transactions to the trade repository authorised for
the purpose by the Reserve Bank on a daily basis by the end of the day, in the form
and manner prescribed by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xi) Exchanges shall furnish any information relating to CDS transactions to the
children.text:
(xi) Exchanges shall furnish any information relating to CDS transactions to the
Reserve Bank or any other agency as may be specified by the Reserve Bank in the
manner and format and within the time frame as may be specified by the Reserve
Bank.

children.title: (i) Market-makers shall put in place appropriate and robust methodologies for marking
children.text:
(i) Market-makers shall put in place appropriate and robust methodologies for marking
to market credit derivative contracts.

children.title: (ii) Market-makers shall use the daily CDS curve published by FIMMDA or any other
children.text:
(ii) Market-makers shall use the daily CDS curve published by FIMMDA or any other
benchmark recommended by FIMMDA, or a proprietary model, to value their CDS
positions.

children.title: (iii) Market-makers using their proprietary model for valuing the CDS positions shall
children.text:
(iii) Market-makers using their proprietary model for valuing the CDS positions shall
disclose the valuation as per the proprietary model, including the rationale for using
that model and an explanation of the valuation methodology in the Notes to
Accounts in their financial statements. The disclosure shall also include the
valuation as per the CDS curve published by FIMMDA or a benchmark
recommended by FIMMDA.2

hierarchy.title: 12. Prudential norms, accounting and capital requirements
hierarchy.text: 12. Prudential norms, accounting and capital requirements

children.title: (i) Market participants shall follow the applicable prudential norms and capital
children.text:
(i) Market participants shall follow the applicable prudential norms and capital
adequacy requirements for credit derivatives issued by their respective regulators.

children.title: (ii) The accounting of credit derivative contracts by market participants shall be as per
children.text:
(ii) The accounting of credit derivative contracts by market participants shall be as per
notified and applicable accounting standards read with regulatory
guidelines/instructions issued by the respective regulators. In case the notified
applicable accounting standards or the respective regulator have not prescribed the
accounting treatment for credit derivative contracts, guidance, if any, issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India shall be followed in this regard.

hierarchy.title: 13. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank
hierarchy.text: 13. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank

children.title: (i) The Reserve Bank may call for any information or statement or seek any
children.text:
(i) The Reserve Bank may call for any information or statement or seek any
clarification, which in the opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant, from persons or
agencies dealing in credit derivatives contracts, including eligible participants, and

2 The requirement to disclose valuation as per the CDS curve published by FIMMDA or a benchmark recommended by FIMMDA shall be effective once FIMMDA starts publishing the CDS curve or recommends a valuation benchmark.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
such persons, agencies and participants shall furnish such information, statement
or clarification in the manner and format and within the time frame as may be
specified by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (i) The Reserve Bank, or any other person or agency authorised by the Reserve Bank,
children.text:
(i) The Reserve Bank, or any other person or agency authorised by the Reserve Bank,
may in public interest, publish any anonymised data related to transactions in credit
derivatives market.

children.title: (i) In the event of any person or agency violating any provision of these Directions or
children.text:
(i) In the event of any person or agency violating any provision of these Directions or
the provisions of any other applicable law, the Reserve Bank may, in addition to
taking any penal or regulatory action in accordance with law, disallow that person
or agency from dealing in the credit derivatives market for a period not exceeding
one month at a time, after providing reasonable opportunity to the person or agency
to defend its actions, and such action will be made public by the Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 16. These Directions shall apply to all credit derivative transactions entered into from
hierarchy.text:
16. These Directions shall apply to all credit derivative transactions entered into from
the date the Directions come into effect. Existing Directions will continue to be
applicable to the credit derivatives transactions undertaken in accordance with the said
Directions till the expiry of those contracts.
